title: siRNA Electroporation of Hydra
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5n0tg","text":"1. siRNA Design","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":3,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"24qek","text":"· find 21 bp sequence in coding region starting with AA dinucleotide","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cef8t","text":"· GC contents between 30-50% seem to be more effective than those with higher GC contents","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ftms2","text":"· avoid poly(T) sequences (more than 3 nucleotides) since this serves as termination signal for RNA pol III","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a2akd","text":"· design 3-4 siRNAs targeting different loci within the gene of interestà combination of several siRNAs may result in better knock down efficiencies ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a0e3j","text":"· check for off targets using the hydra 2.0 database ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9a93m","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aepmo","text":"Some siRNAs work better than others to knock down gene expression, we used a mix of 3 siRNAs in the electroporations.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f8b87","text":"","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cgg0e","text":"2. steps: Wash animals (fed one day earlier) 5 times with Milli-Q water and incubate for 45 minutes at 18°C
Place 20 animals into electroporation cuvetter with 0.4cm gap and remove as much liquid as possible.
Add 200 µL sterilized 10mM HEPES (pH7.0) containing 4µM siRNA
Tap cuvette 10 times to evenly distribute animals, leave animals for 5 minutes to allow them to relax and extend.
Set Biorad GenePulser Xcell electroporation system to the following: Voltage: 150 V Length: 50 ms Number of Pulses: 2 Pulse Intervals: 0.1 s
Electroporate animals. Immediately after administering each pulse, add 500 µL 18°C restoration medium.
Carefully transfer animals to a dish filled with restoration medium
Let animals recover in restoration medium for 48 hours.
creator: Adrienne Cho
